Bookmark rules: Maintain the bookmark‑based insertion logic; no other functional changes are required.
Replace the non‑existent BookmarkCollection.Exists call with a null‑check on the indexer (templateDoc.Range.Bookmarks[bookmarkName] == null).
Table rules: Locate the table with GetChild(NodeType.Table, 0, true) and cast to Table.
When removing a column, iterate over each Row and call Cells[index].Remove() after checking the cell count.
Use Cell.CellFormat.Shading to set background colour – Row does not expose a Shading property.
Guard against a missing table by using FirstOrDefault() instead of direct index access.
When using LINQ with .Where((row, index) => ...) the index must correspond to the product list after the header is skipped.
Range rules: Keep placeholder replacement logic unchanged; Range.Replace works for plain‑text templates.
Document merge/insert rules: When inserting another document, specify ImportFormatMode (e.g., KeepSourceFormatting) to control style handling.
Removed the non‑existent Aspose.Words.Import namespace; NodeImporter resides directly in Aspose.Words.
Remove the non‑existent namespace import: `using Aspose.Words.Import;` – `ImportFormatOptions` lives in `Aspose.Words`.
Remove or correct the SmartStyleBehavior assignment – ImportFormatOptions does not expose a boolean property; the import mode already controls style handling.
Keep using ImportFormatMode.KeepSourceFormatting when source styles are required, otherwise use ImportFormatMode.UseDestinationStyles.
Loading rules: Do not import non‑existent namespaces (e.g., Aspose.Words.LoadOptions). The LoadOptions class resides directly in Aspose.Words.
Use the Document(Stream) constructor to load a WORDML string via a MemoryStream – this follows the lifecycle rule of disposing streams with using.
Add the missing namespace Aspose.Words.Loading to access LoadOptions.
Create a LoadOptions instance and pass it to the Document constructor that accepts a Stream.
Load the DOCM file with the Document constructor; macros are preserved automatically.
Use an object initializer to set LoadOptions.LoadFormat instead of passing LoadFormat to the constructor (the constructor expects a string in some versions).
Keep the using directives for Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Loading, and Aspose.Words.Saving.
Add the missing using directive for Aspose.Words.Loading to bring LoadOptions into scope.
Ensure the Merge method signature matches IDocumentMergerPlugin: LoadOptions[] (from Aspose.Words.Loading).
Saving/Export rules: Keep the rest of the logic unchanged; the MHTML save options are correct.
Use a verbatim string literal (prefixed with @) for multi‑line Markdown text.
Save the document with Document.Save(string) – the format is inferred from the file extension.
If you need to limit pages, use PageIndex and PageCount on ImageSaveOptions.
Keep using ImageSaveOptions with SaveFormat.Tiff and set Resolution/ImageSize as required.
Instantiate ImageSaveOptions with the SaveFormat.Png constructor.
Use the correct SaveFormat enum from Aspose.Words (no extra using needed beyond Aspose.Words).
Add a reference to the Aspose.Words NuGet package (dotnet add package Aspose.Words) so that Document, ImageSaveOptions, SaveFormat and MultiPageLayout are available.
Use ImageSaveOptions with SaveFormat.Tiff and set PageLayout to MultiPageLayout.TiffFrames() to produce a multi‑frame TIFF.
Use the correct property name 'MultiPageLayout' on ImageSaveOptions (not 'PageLayout').
Use ImageSaveOptions with SaveFormat.Tiff for TIFF conversion.
Save the result with doc.Save – use SaveFormat.Docm if you need to keep macros, otherwise DOCX is fine.
Save the final document with the desired format (Docx in this example).
Use the `InsertHtml(string html, HtmlInsertOptions options)` overload; `HtmlInsertOptions` resides in the `Aspose.Words` namespace.
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Remove unused using directives (e.g., System.Xml, Aspose.Words.Drawing) to keep the code clean.
Keep the using directives for System.Drawing, System.Linq and Aspose.Words namespaces.
Layout rules: Assign the layout using MultiPageLayout.TiffFrames() which creates a multi‑frame TIFF.
Keep the required namespaces: `Aspose.Words` for core types and `Aspose.Words.Saving` for `BreakType`.
